## 2025-05-15 - [XSS in HTML Email Generation]
**Vulnerability:** Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in HTML email generation via RSS feeds and AI summaries.
**Learning:** Even when content is generated by an AI (like Llama 3.3), it must be treated as untrusted user input. RSS feeds from external sources are also significant XSS vectors. Protocol validation (blocking `javascript:`) is necessary but should be case-insensitive. HTML attribute escaping (with `quote=True`) is required to prevent attribute injection. Dynamic values used in `id` or `href` (anchors) must be sanitized or escaped.
**Prevention:** Always use `html.escape()` for all dynamic content inserted into HTML templates. Validate URL protocols (case-insensitively) and escape URLs before placing them in attributes. Sanitize dynamic IDs/anchors using a allow-list approach (e.g., regex `[^a-z0-9\-]`).
